title: Your favorite shows on your keychain
body: ![](http://farm3.static.flickr.com/2017/1831414391_d633d9f562_m.jpg)I had already heard about the possibility of installing programs on a USB flash drive (or other flash memory device) so you can run them directly, regardless of which computer you plug it into. But I hadn’t tried it until today, and I actually find it very useful since, for example, you can customize your **Firefox with your favorite plugins**, set up an FTP client with your accounts configured, or simply have an image editor, word processor, spreadsheet, HTML editor, etc., right on the same device where you store your most important documents. Who hasn’t had the experience of not being able to open or edit a file because the computer didn’t have the right program for it? At [PortableApps.com](http://portableapps.com/), you can find these programs, as well as a suite with a menu where you can manage all the content on your USB flash drive.
